L1.area: Image format parsing and decoding plugins

L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists where an unvalidated 32-bit integer value read from a crafted image file is used directly as a loop bound, resulting in an OverflowError on 32-bit systems or process termination via out-of-memory on 64-bit systems.

L3.area: OLE-based image format parsing and property extraction
L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in an OLE-based image format parser where an unvalidated 32-bit integer representing the number of color bands is read from a crafted image file and used directly as a loop bound, resulting in an OverflowError on 32-bit systems or process termination via out-of-memory on 64-bit systems.

ground_truth.reason: The image parser reads a 32-bit integer from a crafted file and passes it directly to range() without validation, causing an OverflowError on 32-bit Python or OOM-based denial of service on 64-bit Python.
